source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design one representative 12 m clear-span simple-supported pedestrian footbridge with two parallel steel wide-flange stringers and a transverse timber deck, fully compliant with the EWB-USA Technical Guidelines v4 (2023) Footbridge chapter. The bridge is an explicit multi-part assembly and must be modeled as such: the deliverable is a full assembly STEP AP242 with distinct solid bodies for every member family, not a monolithic shell. The major part families are (A) steel main stringers, (B) transverse timber deck planks, (C) steel cross-bracing, (D) timber handrail framing (posts, top/mid rails, balusters), (E) steel connection brackets/angles and fasteners between timber and steel members, and (F) elastomeric bearing pads. Two W310x45 (W12x30) ASTM A992 steel stringers (fy = 345 MPa), 12 m long, simply supported on reinforced-concrete abutments with elastomeric pad bearings, run parallel 1.5 m apart. Transverse timber deck planks are 50 mm x 150 mm pressure-treated softwood (allowable bending stress Fb = 10 MPa, allowable shear Fv = 1.0 MPa), spanning 1.5 m between stringers, spaced 400 mm on center in the span direction. A timber handrail 1100 mm tall with vertical balusters 100 mm clear between runs on both sides is connected to deck joists with steel angle brackets. Intermediate cross-bracing is placed every 3 m to stabilize the stringer pair. The bridge carries a uniformly distributed pedestrian live load of 3.6 kPa over a 1.5 m clear walkway width, self-weight of deck + stringers + railings, and the EWB-USA climatic wind pressure of 1.0 kPa applied laterally on the exposed envelope. A pedestrian load factor and a 2.0 safety factor on timber stresses per EWB-USA apply; steel is checked per AISC 360 ASD with typical Omega = 1.67 on bending. Interfaces are explicit and must be modeled with realistic fastener/bearing stiffness: stringer-to-deck is shear-transferred through deck screws / joist hangers at each plank crossing; handrail-post-to-deck is bolted through angle brackets; cross-bracing is bolted to stringer webs; stringer ends rest on bearing pads that rest on the concrete abutments. Deliverables: (a) STEP AP242 assembly with one body per stringer, one body per deck plank, one body per handrail component, one body per bracing member, and separate bodies for brackets and bearing pads, each tagged with part_family (A..F) and instance_index; (b) linear static structural FEA per three independent EWB-USA load cases LC1 live, LC2 wind, LC3 combined live+wind+dead, reporting per-part max stress + assembly-level deflection + connection demand/capacity ratio at each stringer-to-deck and bracing-to-stringer joint. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required (stringer LTB is envelope-checked by AISC lookup, not a separate eigenvalue). The multipart FEM check is performed on the assembled model; per-part stress is reported but does not itself gate pass/fail.
short_prompt:
Design a 12 m pedestrian timber-steel footbridge as a multi-part assembly per the EWB-USA Technical Guidelines v4 (2023).

Two W310x45 A992 steel stringers (fy 345 MPa) span the 12 m clear; treated-timber deck planks, crossbeams, and railing; cast-in-place RC abutments.

Load cases: LC1 pedestrian live 3.6 kPa on the deck; LC2 wind 1.0 kPa lateral on the envelope; LC3 self-weight.

Pass/fail: stringer no yield with SF, timber with SF 2 on bending Fb 10 MPa and shear Fv 1.0 MPa; midspan deflection <= L/400; FEM at assembled level; bolted and welded connection DCR <= 1.0.

prompt.title: 12 m EWB-USA pedestrian footbridge (steel + timber) (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 12 m simple-span pedestrian footbridge: two W310x45 steel stringers, transverse 50 mm timber deck planks on 400 mm spacing, timber handrails on both sides, cross-bracing every 3 m, steel angle brackets, and elastomeric bearing pads on concrete abutments. Carries 3.6 kPa live + 1.0 kPa wind + dead per EWB-USA Technical Guidelines v4 with timber SF 2 and AISC 360 ASD for steel. FEM pass/fail is set on the assembled model; part-level stress is reported per instance.

parts.name: steel_main_stringer
parts.role: longitudinal W310x45 (W12x30) ASTM A992 main beam spanning the 12 m clear opening; pair carries live load + dead + wind

geometric_constraints.support_condition: simply supported on abutments via bearing pads

parts.material_hint: ASTM A992 steel, fy=345 MPa, E=200 GPa

parts.name: timber_deck_plank
parts.role: transverse plank spanning 1.5 m between stringers, forms walking surface
parts.material_hint: pressure-treated softwood, Fb=10 MPa, Fv=1.0 MPa

parts.name: cross_bracing_member
parts.role: intermediate lateral bracing between the two stringers at 3 m spacing to stabilize against wind and prevent stringer LTB rotation

geometric_constraints.configuration: X or K between stringer webs

parts.material_hint: ASTM A992 or A36 small rolled section / angle

parts.name: handrail_component
parts.role: timber handrail framing (posts, top rail, mid rails, balusters) on both sides of the walkway; 1100 mm tall with 100 mm baluster clear

parts.name: steel_angle_bracket_and_fastener
parts.role: steel angle / joist hanger / screw / bolt connecting timber deck and handrails to steel stringers

geometric_constraints.typ_size: L2x2x1/4 angle or equivalent joist hanger; deck screws 1/4 in; handrail bolts 3/8 in
geometric_constraints.count_note: at least one bracket per deck-plank-to-stringer crossing and one per handrail post

parts.name: elastomeric_bearing_pad
parts.role: neoprene bearing pad at each stringer-to-abutment interface; provides rotation and differential movement
parts.material_hint: 60 durometer neoprene, typical bridge bearing pad

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: x = span (12 m), y = transverse width (1.5 m walkway + railings), z = vertical

load_cases.name: pedestrian_live
load_cases.application: uniform on 1.5 m x 12 m deck

boundary_conditions.abutment_bearings: simple supports (pinned one end, roller other) on elastomeric pads
boundary_conditions.stringer_to_deck: shear-transfer through deck screws / joist hangers at each plank crossing; model as tied or coupled with screw-pair equivalent stiffness
boundary_conditions.cross_bracing_to_stringer: bolted at each stringer web; model as coupled at the bolt axis
boundary_conditions.handrail_to_deck: bolted through angle brackets at post base; model as coupled pair

objective.primary: pass all EWB-USA criteria on the assembled model

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 0.6 * fy = 0.6 * 345 MPa AISC ASD bending envelope
multipart acceptance is set on the assembled FEA; per-part stress is reported but not gated

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded/seated (elastomeric bearing pad under stringer end)
interfaces.load_path: vertical reaction + longitudinal thermal expansion from stringer into abutment

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted/screwed (E-family deck screws or joist hangers)
interfaces.load_path: deck bending reaction and pedestrian load into stringer top flange
lateral wind + stringer LTB restraint between stringer webs

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted (E-family angle brackets at post base)
interfaces.load_path: handrail horizontal line load + baluster moment into deck / rim joist

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A 12 m pedestrian footbridge is never a monolithic shell: two steel stringers, dozens of timber planks, handrail frames, bracing, brackets and bearing pads are physically and code-wise distinct fabricated parts connected by defined interfaces.
notes.speculation_caveats: Counts for timber planks, handrail components, and steel brackets are inferred from the described 400 mm on-center plank spacing, 3 m bracing interval, and typical handrail post spacing; original sample does not enumerate individual part counts.
